explanations.15: This tells Docker to wait until the database container reports it's healthy before starting the Joomla container, preventing connection errors on startup.
explanations.16: This uses your computer's user ID and group ID (or defaults to 1000 if not set) so files created inside the container belong to your user account instead of root.
explanations.27: This runs a health check command inside the container every few seconds; if it succeeds, Docker knows the database is ready to accept connections.
explanations.30: Like line 16, this ensures the database container runs with your user's permissions so you can edit database files without permission problems.
